MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Arthur Park and Kevin Solan for Platte River Insurance. Brian McCaffrey (Litigation Counsel) for Thomas Lucas. Andrew Schultz for Thomas Lucas (Trustee). Ernest Der for Leobard Cameron.

The Court noted that Mr. Hill was not present. Mr. Schultz asked the Court to enter a default, stated that there was no need for an evidentiary hearing, that Mr. Hill had not responded, that he was asking for $1,351,568.00 (principal amount of refinance only), that he was dropping the request for the fair market value of the foreclosed Oakland property, that he would be asking for attorney's fees in the amount of $8,267.00 and costs in the amount of $352.50, damages and punitive damages. Mr. Solan stated that he did not oppose Mr. Schultz' request for surcharge against Mr. Hill. Off Calendar Jan-05-2010 Continued To Jan-27-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Thomas Lucas. Arthur Park for Platte River Insurance Company.

Mr. Siracusa stated that he was about to file an action against the lenders with regard to the encumbrances on the property but that one of the lenders was no longer in business, that he wanted to proceed with the petition and that he believed they were entitled to double damages. In addition, he stated that the amount was $1,351,568.00 without the double damages, that the property had a carry value at $325,000.00, that he could get an appraisal and that he could submit a declaration setting forth the amount of legal fees incurred to date. Mr. Park stated that he wanted to see how the damages were calculated, and that he would ask for reimbursement if successful with the 850 petition if and when they had to pay out the premium. Mr. Siracusa stated that they were paying mortgages on two properties and that there were no funds to pay for Mr. Cameron's care. The Court stated that and evidentiary hearing would be needed and asked if Counsel could have an indemnity agreement. Mr. Siracusa will provide the information regarding the calculations to Mr. Park and Counsel agreed to enter into a stipulation of facts. The matter was continued to January 27, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status.
